_comment_purpose: markdownlint-cli2 config for stealth-vps. We keep structural rules on (heading spacing, list spacing, code-fence languages, no-bare-urls) and disable the cosmetic ones that don't affect rendered output or that conflict with the project's prose style.
_comment_MD013: line-length: disabled. Tables and prose in this project intentionally use long lines (one row = one line). Wrapping would hurt readability of the rendered output.
_comment_MD060: table-column-style: disabled. Some tables use compact pipe spacing (no space-around-pipe) and others use loose; both render identically on GitLab + GitHub. The rule flags style inconsistency, but our tables are correct GFM tables.
_comment_MD024: no-duplicate-heading: allow siblings (different parent sections). CHANGELOG entries repeat '### Added', '### Changed', '### Fixed' under each release header — that's the Keep-a-Changelog convention.
_comment_MD041: first-line-heading: disabled. Some files (e.g. docs/internal/roadmap-v0.6-v0.7.md) intentionally start with a comment-style intro before the H1.
_comment_MD033:
no-inline-html: allow. We use ,
, , and a couple of
for readability in tables.
_comment_MD036: no-emphasis-as-heading: disabled. Some docs use **bold** lines for sub-sub-section emphasis where adding a fifth-level heading would be heavier than the content warrants.
_comment_kept: Kept-on (gating): MD022 blanks-around-headings, MD031 blanks-around-fences, MD032 blanks-around-lists, MD040 fenced-code-language, MD034 no-bare-urls, MD029 ol-prefix. Those are structural and easy to fix when they fire.
